name: SOAP
description: SOAP (Separate, Orient All, Permute) is an intermediate 2x2 speedsolving method. It breaks the solve into three clear phases: separating pieces into their correct layers, orienting all pieces, and then permuting them into their final positions. This structured approach makes it accessible while still being competitive.

steps.name: Separate
steps.description: Separate the pieces so that each layer contains only the pieces that belong to it. Top-layer pieces go to the top and bottom-layer pieces go to the bottom, regardless of their orientation or exact position.
tips: Focus only on which layer each piece belongs to, not its orientation
Practice recognizing which pieces need to be swapped between layers

steps.name: Orient All
steps.description: Orient all pieces on both layers so that the top and bottom face colors are correct. After this step, both faces should show a solid color, though pieces may not be in their correct positions.
tips: Learn the orientation algorithms for both layers
Some cases allow orienting both layers simultaneously
Recognition speed improves significantly with practice

steps.name: Permute
steps.description: Permute all pieces on both layers to reach the solved state. Since everything is already oriented, this reduces to a PBL (Permutation of Both Layers) step.
tips: PBL algorithms are shared with the Ortega method
Learn to recognize permutation cases quickly by checking adjacent sticker pairs
Some lucky cases skip this step entirely

pros: Shares PBL algorithms with Ortega method
Good balance of speed and ease of learning
cons: Higher move count than one-look methods like CLL or EG
Three-step process means more pauses than two-step methods
Not commonly used at the highest competitive level
Separation step can be awkward for certain scrambles
